metadata.name: cow_settlement
metadata.description: CoW Protocol settlement program. HAND-WRITTEN IDL: this program is a native/Pinocchio program, not built with the Anchor framework, so this file was authored manually to describe its wire format as closely as the Anchor IDL grammar allows. See the per-instruction/type docs for spots where the on-chain format cannot be fully expressed (non-standard 1-byte instruction discriminators, and BeginSettle's dynamically-shaped tail).

instructions.name: initialize
docs: Creates the singleton settlement state PDA. Succeeds only once.

Non-standard discriminator: this program uses a single instruction-selector byte (see SettlementInstruction in the Rust source), not Anchor's usual 8-byte sighash. The `discriminator` below reflects the real on-chain bytes.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Funds the state PDA's rent and pays the transaction fee.

accounts.name: state_pda
docs: Canonical PDA seeded by the literal string "settlement".

instructions.name: create_buffer
docs: Creates one or more per-token buffer PDAs (SPL token accounts) in a single instruction.

IDL MODELING NOTE: the real instruction accepts an unbounded number of (buffer_pda, mint) pairs as remaining accounts, one pair per buffer, with at least one pair required (CreateBuffer rejects zero buffers). Anchor's IDL grammar has no 'repeated group' construct, so this IDL instead declares up to 15 indexed pairs (buffer_pda_0/mint_0 .. buffer_pda_14/mint_14), matching this deployment's largest expected batch. buffer_pda_0/mint_0 are unconditionally present; the rest are marked optional and are only present up to however many buffers the specific transaction actually creates. A transaction creating more than 15 buffers in one instruction (unlikely given transaction size/CU limits) would have trailing accounts this IDL doesn't name.
Each buffer_pda_i must be the canonical PDA for seeds ["settlement", mint_i, "buffer"].

accounts.name: buffer_pda_0
docs: Guaranteed present: CreateBuffer rejects an instruction with zero buffers.

accounts.name: mint_0
docs: Guaranteed present: CreateBuffer rejects an instruction with zero buffers.

accounts.name: buffer_pda_1
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 2 buffers.

accounts.name: mint_1
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 2 buffers.

accounts.name: buffer_pda_2
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 3 buffers.

accounts.name: mint_2
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 3 buffers.

accounts.name: buffer_pda_3
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 4 buffers.

accounts.name: mint_3
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 4 buffers.

accounts.name: buffer_pda_4
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 5 buffers.

accounts.name: mint_4
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 5 buffers.

accounts.name: buffer_pda_5
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 6 buffers.

accounts.name: mint_5
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 6 buffers.

accounts.name: buffer_pda_6
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 7 buffers.

accounts.name: mint_6
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 7 buffers.

accounts.name: buffer_pda_7
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 8 buffers.

accounts.name: mint_7
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 8 buffers.

accounts.name: buffer_pda_8
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 9 buffers.

accounts.name: mint_8
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 9 buffers.

accounts.name: buffer_pda_9
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 10 buffers.

accounts.name: mint_9
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 10 buffers.

accounts.name: buffer_pda_10
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 11 buffers.

accounts.name: mint_10
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 11 buffers.

accounts.name: buffer_pda_11
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 12 buffers.

accounts.name: mint_11
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 12 buffers.

accounts.name: buffer_pda_12
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 13 buffers.

accounts.name: mint_12
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 13 buffers.

accounts.name: buffer_pda_13
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 14 buffers.

accounts.name: mint_13
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 14 buffers.

accounts.name: buffer_pda_14
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 15 buffers.

accounts.name: mint_14
docs: Present only if the instruction creates at least 15 buffers.

instructions.name: create_order
docs: Allocates a per-order PDA and writes the initial OrderAccount body.

order_pda's canonical seeds are ["settlement", sha256(intent_bytes), "order"]. This is not expressible as a static `pda` entry because the middle seed is a hash of the entire `intent` argument, not a plain field/account reference, which is outside what the Anchor PDA-seed grammar (const / arg / account) can describe.

accounts.name: owner
docs: Must match intent.owner; authenticates the order.

accounts.name: created_by
docs: Funds the new order PDA's rent.

accounts.name: order_pda
docs: See the seed-derivation note above; cannot be auto-derived by IDL-driven tooling.

instructions.name: begin_settle
docs: Pulls funds for a batch of orders. Must be paired in the same transaction with a FinalizeSettle at `finalize_ix_index`.

IDL LIMITATION: only the fixed-size prefix (finalize_ix_index) is represented as a typed argument. After it, the real wire format is `[order_count: u8][bump; order_count][transfer_count; order_count][amount: u64 BE; sum(transfer_count)]` — a hand-packed layout with no Borsh length prefixes and a trailing array whose length depends on the sum of an earlier array. This has no representation in the Anchor/Borsh/IDL type grammar (no vec/array type can express 'shared count governs several sibling arrays' or 'length = sum of another field'), so it is intentionally left out of `args` rather than mislabeled as `bytes` (which would imply a Borsh Vec<u8> length prefix that isn't actually present and would make a generic decoder misparse it). Anchor/Solscan-style tooling will only decode the discriminator and finalize_ix_index for this instruction; the remainder needs bespoke client logic.

Per-order accounts follow the 3 shared accounts below as a repeated remaining-accounts group: [order_pda (readonly), sell_token_account (writable), destination (writable) x transfer_count], sorted by ascending order_pda address.

accounts.name: state_pda
docs: Must be the canonical state PDA; signs each pull as the user's SPL delegate.

args.name: finalize_ix_index
docs: Index of the paired FinalizeSettle in this transaction. Little-endian, matching standard Borsh/Anchor u16 decoding.

instructions.name: finalize_settle
docs: Validates that a BeginSettle at `begin_ix_index` exists and points back at this instruction. Must not be called via CPI.
Non-standard discriminator, see the note on `initialize`.

args.name: begin_ix_index
docs: Index of the paired BeginSettle in this transaction. Little-endian, matching standard Borsh/Anchor u16 decoding.

types.name: OrderAccount
docs: Body of an order PDA, created by create_order. 200 bytes total: 1-byte discriminator + 199 bytes of fields below.

types.name: SettlementState
docs: Body of the singleton settlement state PDA. Carries no fields beyond the 1-byte discriminator; its existence and address are what matter.

types.name: OrderIntent
docs: Canonical 150-byte order intent. Also the exact bytes hashed (SHA-256) to produce the order UID used in the order PDA's seeds, and the exact wire format of create_order's `intent` argument. Field order and encoding here are load-bearing: they must match this program's Rust definition exactly.

errors.name: FinalizeBeforeInitialize
errors.msg: The FinalizeSettle included as input to BeginSettle isn't before the actual BeginSettle index.

errors.name: BeginFinalizePairOverlap
errors.msg: Another BeginSettle/FinalizeSettle of this program appears strictly between this pair's bounds, nesting or overlapping two settlements.

errors.name: MissingCounterpartInstruction
errors.msg: The counterpart index points past the end of the transaction's instruction list, so no instruction sits there.

errors.name: CounterpartIsExternal
errors.msg: The instruction at the counterpart index belongs to a different program.

errors.name: InvalidCounterpartDiscriminator
errors.msg: The counterpart instruction's discriminator byte couldn't be recovered from its data.

errors.name: InvalidCounterpartCounterpart
errors.msg: The counterpart instruction's own counterpart index couldn't be recovered from its data.

errors.name: MismatchedCounterpartDiscriminator
errors.msg: The counterpart's discriminator isn't the expected BeginSettle/FinalizeSettle kind, or its counterpart index doesn't point back at this instruction.

errors.name: OwnerMismatch
errors.msg: CreateOrder instruction wasn't signed by the created OrderIntent owner.

errors.name: OrderNotCanonical
errors.msg: A BeginSettle order account doesn't sit at the canonical order PDA derived from the intent it stores and the supplied bump.

errors.name: OrdersNotStrictlyIncreasing
errors.msg: BeginSettle's order accounts aren't passed strictly increasing by address.

errors.name: SellTokenAccountMismatch
errors.msg: A BeginSettle sell token account doesn't match the sell_token_account recorded in the order's intent.

errors.name: SellTokenAccountInvalid
errors.msg: A BeginSettle sell token account isn't a valid SPL token account (wrong data length or not owned by the token program).

errors.name: SellTokenOwnerMismatch
errors.msg: A BeginSettle sell token account's SPL owner isn't the order's intent owner.

errors.name: AccountCountNotMatchingOrderCount
errors.msg: BeginSettle's order-account count doesn't match the structure its instruction data expects.

errors.name: CalledViaCpi
errors.msg: BeginSettle or FinalizeSettle was invoked via CPI rather than as a top-level transaction instruction.

errors.name: OrderCancelled
errors.msg: A BeginSettle order has been cancelled by its owner and can no longer be settled.

errors.name: OrderExpired
errors.msg: A BeginSettle order's valid_to lies in the past: the order has expired and can no longer be settled.

errors.name: TransferCountMismatch
errors.msg: The transfer counts in BeginSettle don't sum to the number of transfer amounts, so destinations and amounts can't be paired up exactly.

errors.name: StateAccountMismatch
errors.msg: BeginSettle's state account isn't the canonical settlement state PDA, which must sign the pulls as the user's token delegate.
